RULING:
Defendant Dowman Products, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties John Dobrocke, et al.

RULING:
Defendant Bayer Cropscience, Inc. SUCCESSOR TO AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC.'s motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents and sanctions. Argued and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant's motion to compel production of documents is granted and its request for sanctions is denied. Plaintiffs must provide the responsive documents (and if desired by them with redactions for claimed work product and a privilege log) either in electronic or hard copy form without cost to defendant. Because the redactions could be done electronically, plaintiffs' request for 15 cents per page is essentially a request to be paid for the time and effort spent doing the redactions. This is not a recoverable expense. (The Citation is stricken). Defendant's request for sanctions is denied because the issue raised by the motion had previously been unsettled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Gilbert L. Purcell, Brayton Purcell LLC, (415) 898-1555 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Trung Quach, Esq. of McKenna Long & Aldridge LLP (415) 267-4094 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: (Motion for Summary Judgment withdrawn.) Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Motion denied as to 1st cause of action for negligence. Motion granted as to 2nd cause of action for strict liability, 3rd cause of action for false representation, 5th cause of action for premises liability, and punitive damages request. Motion denied as to 4th Cause of action for loss of consortium. (The court adds: Zwicker factually different; the injury is the crux of when the loss of consortium should accrue.) Motion conceded as to false representation. No triable issue of fact regarding strict liability, no evidence that defendant sold or supplied asbestos containing products that defendant was exposed to. No evidence that defendant controlled the premises or supervised plaintiff. Court does not rule on transcripts from prior cases in view of their insufficiency to raise a triable issue of fact regarding punitive damages. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.)
